characters.name: Airman Endo
characters.description: Airman EndoAirman EndoBasic informationGender:MaleOccupation:Enlisted Man, USAFDescription:Airman Endo, and enlisted man at Buckley US Airforce Base, outside of Denver.Character informationAppeared on:Mork & MindyPortrayed by:Ty HendersonEpisodes appeared in:The Mork Syndrome Airman Endois on of the enlisted men Mork meets when he goes toBuckley Airforce Baseoutside Denver, thinking the USAF is like a variation of Club Med in Season 2'sThe Mork Syndrome.  He is played byTy Henderson. Endo is tall, good looking, African American enlisted man in the USAF, who with his buddies is trying to escape the cold of the onset of a Colorado Winter at Buckley Airforce base, and complaining about the food and coffee on the base and hoping whatever secret location they are being sent to that night has a McDonalds nearby. He is the first one to spot Mork coming out of the latrine, and thanks to the uniform Mork has swiped mistakes him for a USAF Lieutenant.

He is played byAlan McRae. Endo is tall, good looking, sandy haired enlisted man in the USAF, who with his buddy Airman Endo, and others, is trying to escape the cold of the onset of a Colorado Winter at Buckley Airforce base, and complaining about the food and coffee on the base and hoping whatever secret location they are being sent to that night has a McDonalds nearby.

characters.name: Al Klevins
characters.description: Al KlevinsAl Kelvins, Mindy and Frank Bickley's LandlordBasic informationGender:MaleOccupation:LandlordDescription:Al KelvinsCharacter informationAppeared on:Mork & MindyPortrayed by:Larry GelmanEpisodes appeared in:Mindy, Mindy, Mindy Al Kelvinsis the owner of1619 Pine Street, and the Landlord of both Mindy McConnell and Franklin Bickley, who appears in the episodeMindy, Mindy, Mindy, and is played byLarry Gelman. Like the DaVinci's, Al Klevins is a native of The Bronx, NY. who up stakes and moved to Boulder, where he has purchased and rents out several properties. While Mindy is away for the weekend to cover the 'Winter Wheat Conference' in Topeka, Kansas, Bickley drops up to let Mork know that his and Mindy's landlord, Al Klevins, will be dropping by to install smoke detectors in their apartments, and on noticing the state of the apartment, Mork slowly melting down without Mindy, tells Mork he better get the place in shape. Klevins, he informs him is a creep and has tossed tenants out for having their doormat crooked.

characters.name: Althea
characters.description: AltheaAlthea, school mate of Mindy & Glenda FayeBasic informationGender:FemaleDescription:AltheaCharacter informationAppeared on:Mork & MindyPortrayed by:Marilyn KaganEpisodes appeared in:Long Before We Met Altheais a fellow alumni ofBoulder High School, alongsideMindyandGlenda Faye, who they meet up with again at their reunion in the Season 4 episodeLong Before We Met. She was played byMarilyn Kagan. Althea arrives at the reunion around 9 months pregnant by her appearance, and vastly enthusiastic.  She is elated to see both Mindy and Glenda and treats them both as old friends bouncing around first with them and then Mork, before realizing she doesn't know who he is.  Mork introduces himself to her as Mindy's better half, but when Mindy has to make the return introduction she can't, and is forced to address her pregnancy instead.

characters.name: Angry Soldier
characters.description: Angry SoldierSoldier who is part of Exidon's elite bodyguardBasic informationGender:MaleOccupation:BodyguardDescription:The Angry Soldier is a part of King Exidon's bodyguard who turns against him when he discovers the truth, from Mandy and Mork, about what his ruler has been up to in extorting the people.Character informationAppeared on:Mork & MindyPortrayed by:Tyler HornEpisodes appeared in:Mork in Wonderland, Part 2 The Angry Soldieris a member of KingExidon's elite bodyguard on the planetMirth, who appears inMork in Wonderland, Part 2.  He is played byTyler Horn. Tall, dark and good looking, clad in the brown uniform and 'exploded chimney' style helmet, the soldier is a member of King Exidon's (the dictator of the planet Mirth) personal body guard.

characters.name: Ann
characters.description: AnnAnn, Lit Teacher & CheerleaderBasic informationGender:FemaleOccupation:Literature Teacher & Pony Express MemberDescription:AnnCharacter informationAppeared on:Mork & MindyPortrayed by:Lorrie MahaffeyEpisodes appeared in:Hold That Mork Annis a member of The Pony Express, the Denver Bronco Cheerleaders, who along with her friend Kathy, take Mork under their wing in his attempt to join their number.  She appears inHold That Morkand is played byLorrie Mahaffey. As Ann points out to Mindy, she is actually a Literature Teacher who happens to also be a cheerleader, rather than the other way around.  It is something she does because she enjoys it, not because she can make a living out of it.   For that reason perhaps she and her friend and co-cheerleader decided to help Mork out on his quest to do something he feels he will enjoy and will be good at, and arrive at Mindy's apartment to help him work out with them.

characters.name: Annie Wanker
characters.description: Annie WankerAnnie Wanker, the wife/widow/wife ofArnold WankerBasic informationGender:FemaleDescription:Annie WankerFamily/personal informationSpouse(s):Arnold WankerCharacter informationAppeared on:Mork & MindyPortrayed by:Fay DeWittEpisodes appeared in:To Tell the Truth Annie Wanker is the (unfortunate) wife of Arnold Wanker the greedy, nasty owner of the premises that Fred McConnell rents to use as the Music Store, and who appears in Season 1's To Tell the Truth, she is played byFay DeWitt. Annie is the wife and, temporarily at least, the widow of Arnold Wanker who is sitting in the car outside as Arnold, in the midst of warning Fred & Mindy that he is intent on driving them out of the store, drops dead in the store.

characters.name: Arnold Wanker
characters.description: Arnold WankerArnold Wanker, Landlord of McConnell's Music Store and all around ScroogeBasic informationGender:MaleFamily/personal informationFamily/Relatives:Annie WankerSpouse(s):Annie WankerCharacter informationAppeared on:Mork & MindyPortrayed by:Logan RamseyEpisodes appeared in:1First appeared in:"To Tell the Truth"in Season 1 (only appearance) Arnold Wankeris theMcConnell's Music Store's tyrannical landlord andFred'sformer classmate whom Mork brought back from the dead in Season 1 episode ofMork and Mindytitled"To Tell The Truth"(episode #8). The part of Mr. Wanker is played in the episode by veteran character actorLogan Ramsey. Wanker appears to be in his early 60s, 5 years old when Cora was a late teenager and babysitting, and an older student when Fred was still playing Little League.

characters.name: Augustus J. Strand
characters.description: Augustus J. StrandAugustus J. Strand, Repair ManBasic informationGender:MaleOccupation:Shop Repair ManDescription:Augustus J. StrandCharacter informationAppeared on:Mork & MindyPortrayed by:Richard LibertiniEpisodes appeared in:Twelve Angry Appliances Augustus J. Strandis the owner of Strand's Repair shop off the Pearl Street Mall in Boulder, who Mindy has a running battle with over his shoddy repair work, and who Mork eventually teaches his own particular brand of lesson, in Season 3's,Twelve Angry Appliances.  He is played byRichard Libertini. Augustus J. Strand is a repair man who has lost all pride in his work, and does the minimum to get things going (if that) just to get the money out of his customers.  When Mindy's portable record player sits down on Mork while he's borrowed it to use it in the Day Care Center with the kids, he thinks he's broken it, until a newly arrived Mindy exasperatedly informs him that she only just got it back from the repair shop for the same problem, and Strand obviously didn't do the job he said he'd done. Unable to understand why Strand wouldn't take pride in his work, as is done Universe wide, Mindy explains to Mork that sometimes people just don't care, especially if it gets them repeat business. But she is determined to get him to fix it properly.

characters.name: Baba Gentle
characters.description: Baba GentleRichard Moll appears as cult leader Baba Gentle in the Season 4 episode "Ailenation" (ep.#9).Character informationAppeared on:Mork and MindyPortrayed by:Richard MollEpisodes appeared in:"Alienation" in Season 4 Baba Gentlewas a character who appears in theSeason 4episode ofMork & Mindytitled"Alienation"(episode #9). The part of Baba Hope is played byRichard Moll, who would later become well known to TV viewers as Bull Shannon on the long-running NBC-TV seriesNight Court, withJohn Larroquette, who plays cult leaderBaba Hopein the episode. WhenMearth, who, in feeling like an outcast, and that he's "unloved" by his parents upon learning fromMorkthat he's a ailen, decides to run away from home, he winds up joining up with a cult called "The Utopians", which is led byBaba Hope(John Larroquette) as he's convinced that the cult really loves him, as Mork, posing as "Baba Loo", and Mindy both infiltrate the cult to try to retrive him. Baba Gentle, who seems rather honest in his beliefs in the cult, but somewhat dim-witted, is ordered by Baba Hope to "train" Mearth on the importance of ridding themselves of their worldly possesions.

characters.name: Baba Hope
characters.description: Baba HopeJohn Larroquette appears as cult leader Baba Hope in the Season 4 episode "Ailenation" (ep.#9).Character informationAppeared on:Mork and MindyPortrayed by:John LarroquetteEpisodes appeared in:"Alienation" in Season 4 Baba Hopewas a character who appears in theSeason 4episode ofMork & Mindytitled"Alienation"(episode #9). The part of Baba Hope is played byJohn Larroquette, who would later become well known to TV viewers as Dan Fielding on the long-running NBC-TV seriesNight Court, a role for which he won four Emmy Awards during the show's run.

characters.name: Bebo
characters.description: BeboBebo, Mork's pet NaugachompBasic informationGender:UnknownDescription:Mork's pet from Ork who zips around the floor at quick pace; Mork is unsure if Bebo is male or femaleFamily/personal informationFamily/Relatives:????Character informationAppeared on:Mork and MindyEpisodes appeared in:21 episodes inSeason 3 Bebois Mork's petNaugachomp, seen throughoutseason 3ofMork and Mindy. As part of The Elder's attempts to re-Orkan, Mork, alongside the transformation of Mindy's attic into an 'Orkan Environment', Mork's pet left behind on Ork, his Naugachomp, Bebo is brought to Earth.   He lives with Mork and Mindy in a specially created 'cage' in Mork's newly refurbished room,

characters.name: Belk
characters.description: BelkBelkBasic informationGender:FemaleOccupation:TribeswomanDescription:Belk, a Stone age woman who is both rescued by and helps Mork & Mindy after their arrival in her time.Character informationAppeared on:Mork & MindyPortrayed by:Chea ColletteEpisodes appeared in:Gotta Run, Part 3 Belkis a stone age cave dweller in jeopardy who Mork and Mindy meet and help after they escape through time to get away from Kalnik in Season 4'sGotta Run, Part 3.She is played byChea Collette. Belk is a young woman being pursued by two males of a rival tribe whose escape takes her across the path a newly arrived Mork & Mindy, fresh out of the time stream.  Her, and her pursuers, appearance help the newly arrived pair ascertain where in time they have arrived.  Until they realize that Belk's pursuers are as aggressive towards them as they are to her.

characters.name: Mr. Bickley
characters.description: Mr. BickleyTom Poston as Mr. BickleyBasic informationGender:MaleOccupation:Freelance Greeting Card WriterDescription:Rather grouchy, irascible type who eventually warms up to Mindy and Mork, and even forms a friendship with Mindy's Dad, Fred. He is never able to find out about Mork being an  Alien, although he's seen the the enormous nest for Mork's egg in the McConnells' apartmentFamily/personal informationFamily/Relatives:Tom Bickley(son)Spouse(s):DivorcedCharacter informationAppeared on:Mork and MindyPortrayed by:Tom PostonEpisodes appeared in:54 in seriesFirst appeared in:Mork the Tolerant Franklin "Frank" Delano Bickleywas a main and recurring character who appeared in 54 of the 95 episodes ofMork and Mindy. The part of Mr. Bickley was played on the series byTom Poston. Mr. Bickley isMindy McConnell's downstairs neighbor, who moves into the building after Mork moves in with Mindy.

characters.name: Big Man
characters.description: Big ManThe Big Man, who Mork tries to make one of his earliest friends.Basic informationGender:MaleDescription:Big ManCharacter informationAppeared on:Mork & MindyPortrayed by:Bruce M. FischerEpisodes appeared in:Mork's Seduction TheBig Manis a customer in theMcConnnell's Music Storethat Mork approaches in his quest to make new friends.  He appears in Season 2'sMork's Seductionand is played byBruce M. Fischer. Much as his name would indicate this target for Mork's friendship is a large guy.  6 foot plus with the build of your average bouncer, and a healthy beard to match, the Big Man is browsing in the McConnell store when he is immediately targeted by Mork on his arrival.  The Orkan on the hunt for more friends, in the wake of Mindy's advice he expand his circle beyond her, so he can experience more of human life.

characters.name: Bill Mason
characters.description: Bill MasonBill Mason, JerkBasic informationGender:MaleDescription:Bill MasonCharacter informationAppeared on:Mork & MindyPortrayed by:Jeff HarlanFirst appeared in:The Mork & Mindy Special Bill Masonplayed byJeff Harlanis a third time date ofMindy's who is, in part, responsible for her meeting Mork inThe Pilot Despite the short duration of his appearance, Bill Mason is possibly one of the most pivotal people ever in Mork & Mindy's lives, as it is his actions that directly lead to their first meeting.

characters.name: Billy
characters.description: BillyCorey Feldman as Billy in the Season 3 episode "Mork the Prankster" (ep.#6).Basic informationGender:MaleDescription:Child who attends the daycare center where Mork worksCharacter informationAppeared on:Mork and MindyPortrayed by:Corey FeldmanEpisodes appeared in:2 inSeason 3First appeared in:"Mork the Prankster"(Season 3 premiere)Last appeared in:"Gunfight at the Mork-Kay Corral"(Season 3) Billyis one of the children who attends the daycare center where Mork works who appears in two episodes ofMork and Mindyin Season 3. The part of Billy is played on the series byCorey Feldman. When Mork and Billy and the other kids at the day care center decide to dress up costume as their favorite characters (Stephaniecomes as Sara Lee,Lolaas Truman Capote). Billy's father wants him to follow in his footsteps as a doctor, but the boy relates to someone with his own name, Billy the Kid.

characters.name: Billy
characters.description: BillyCorey Feldman as Billy in the Season 3 episode "Mork the Prankster" (ep.#6).Basic informationGender:MaleDescription:Child who attends the daycare center where Mork worksCharacter informationAppeared on:Mork and MindyPortrayed by:Corey FeldmanEpisodes appeared in:2 inSeason 3First appeared in:"Mork the Prankster"(Season 3 premiere)Last appeared in:"Gunfight at the Mork-Kay Corral"(Season 3) Billyis one of the children who attends the daycare center where Mork works who appears in two episodes ofMork and Mindyin Season 3. The part of Billy is played on the series byCorey Feldman. When Mork and Billy and the other kids at the day care center decide to dress up costume as their favorite characters (Stephaniecomes as Sara Lee,Lolaas Truman Capote). Billy's father wants him to follow in his footsteps as a doctor, but the boy relates to someone with his own name, Billy the Kid.

characters.name: Billy Vincent
characters.description: Billy VincentBilly VincentBasic informationGender:MaleOccupation:TV ProduderDescription:Billy Vincent, Mork's TV Producer for those 'Amazing Aliens'Character informationAppeared on:Mork & MindyPortrayed by:Stephen StuckerEpisodes appeared in:Gotta Run, Part 3 Billy Vincentis the Producer assigned to Mork's New Show after he has announced to the world that he is an Alien in a final ditch effort to shakeKalnikand his goons who have made the military believe Mork is intent on taking over the world. He appears inGotta Run, Part 3and is played byStephen Stucker Bill is a wildly enthusiastic and flamboyant TV Producer who loves nothing more than to sip white wine and talk concept with his talent.  He is unfortunately not so good with being able to tone things down or back off when its apparent his clients are being pulled three ways from Sunday.   Billy bursts into Mork & Mindy's Hotel Suite in L.A. just after the pair have run the gamut of press and Mork groupies all trying to get a piece of the Alien.  And Mork is already starting to feel the effects of being in demand and on display every moment of his day.  As Mindy tries to soothe and console him, knowing how he feels and urging him to keep calm, Billy arrives in with bevy of show writers in tow.

characters.name: Bob Faith
characters.description: Bob FaithBob Faith, Mirth's Bob Hope parallelBasic informationGender:MaleOccupation:Comedian / GuerillaDescription:Bob FaithCharacter informationAppeared on:Mork & MindyPortrayed by:Ronnie SchellEpisodes appeared in:Mork in WonderlandBob Faithis one of the band of three 'comedians' who is a part ofMandy's guerilla resistance against the tyrannicalExidonon the planetMirth.  He appears inMork in Wonderland, Part 2and is played byRonnie Schell. Bob Faith with his ski shaped nose, pork pie hat, and perennial golf club is Mirth's version ofBob Hope.  Probably the most intelligent of this group of 'Sillies', though not saying much, he is the most dynamic of the group. He has a failing for performing in front of large groups, especially military troops, mirroring his Earthly counterpart's USO work.

characters.name: Bob Miller
characters.description: Bob MillerBob Miller, neighbor of Mork & Mindy and process server.Basic informationGender:MaleOccupation:Process ServerDescription:Bob MillerCharacter informationAppeared on:Mork & MindyPortrayed by:Paul WillsonEpisodes appeared in:Cheerleaders in Chains Bob Milleris a neighbor of Mindy & Mork from across the street who also happens to be a process server. He appears in Season 4'sCheerleaders in Chains, and is played byPaul Willson. Bob arrives at the McConnell's door, shortly after the family has returned with a triumphant Mork, his driver's license finally attained. Mindy answering the door to him, greets him and he introduces himself to her and Mork as one of their neighbors who lives in the house directly opposite.  He's come over, he tells her, because he accidentally got some of her mail.

characters.name: Bob Turnbow
characters.description: Bob TurnbowBob Turnbow, post plastic surgeryBasic informationGender:MaleDescription:Bob Turnbow, an old High School friend of Fred McConnellCharacter informationAppeared on:Mork & MindyPortrayed by:Angus DuncanEpisodes appeared in:Mork's New Look Bob Turnbowis an old High School friend of Fred McConnell's who Mork & Mindy meet when they are out on a double date with Fred and Cathy in Season 3'sMork's New Look.  He is played byAngus Duncan. Bob is a tall strapping, and very young looking for his age, old Boulder High friend of Fred who runs into Fred, Cathy, Mindy and Mork when they are out on a dinner/movie double date, and finishing up their meal in Da Vinci's.

characters.name: Bozz
characters.description: BozzBozzBasic informationGender:FemaleOccupation:'Henchman'Description:Bozz, Wheel's jailbird henchman who works at the Roller Rink in BoulderCharacter informationAppeared on:Mork & MindyPortrayed by:Tom KindleEpisodes appeared in:Dueling SkatesBozzis the jailbird henchman of 'Wheels' Simpson, who appears in Season 3'sDueling Skates, trying to stop Mork from winning his road race with his boss.  He is played byTom Kindle. Bozz is encountered by Mindy & Mork at the Wheels Roller Rink in Boulder when they come looking to speak with Wheels.  Bozz helps run the rink, and is pretty much as uncaring and amoral as his boss.  Noting that at Mork's age he had already served time, he is dismissive of both Mork & MIndy's appeals on behalf of the kids, and echoes all the sarcastic and negative comments that Wheels makes, to the point that Mork wonders if there is a 'negative echo' in the rink. Despite Wheels obvious advantages to the novice Mork, Bozz takes a full part in attempting to further undermine Mork's chances in the road race he challenges Wheel to, to try and save the Center.  Giving Wheels a head start before firing the gun to set Mork going, and laying out rubber tires for Wheels to use to cause Mork further problems down the race circuit.

characters.name: Brad Jackson
characters.description: Brad JacksonBrad JacksonBasic informationGender:MaleOccupation:LawyerDescription:Brad Jackson is an old High School Friend of Mindy's who swings back into town to romance her only for Mork to land in the middle.Character informationAppeared on:Mork & MindyPortrayed by:J.D. HintonEpisodes appeared in:Mork Runs Away Brad Jacksonplayed byJ.D. Hinton, is an oldBoulder High Schoolclassmate ofMindy's that reconnects with her on his return to Boulder, asking her out, only to have an off-putting encounter with Mork inMork Runs Away. Brad, tall, good looking and blond, is an old High school friend of Mindy's who (like her steadySteve Sanders) was on the football team, and who she obviously liked well enough to consider dating, but who never asked her out at the time. Having gone away to Law School, he's doing well for himself and back in town re-connects with her and asks her on a date, not long after Mork's arrival and move in. One that Mork expects to go along on.  Mindy, negotiates Mork's absence by bribing her father with basketball tickets and her date with a regular human male to take Mork out for the evening, so she and Brad can have the place to themselves if they go back there.  Which they do.  The date going well as far as Mindy and Brad are concerned until Mork's unexpected return home. Getting in between them, his frank (and truthful) confessions that he lives with Mindy, gives Brad the wrong idea about Mindy's relationship with him, and her dating practices. Her own attempts to explain don't fare very well and he leaves with the impression she's a swinger.
